I don't think anybody has been yet forced to move from Xfinity to Yahoo, and I read mixed messages whether or not that move will be mandatory, and least for the near future. In any case, they are supposed to give us plenty of advance warning. So, I don't believe that's your problem.

Here are my working Comcast settings. How do they compare to yours?:

Incoming Server Type: IMAP Mail Server Incoming Server Name: imap.comcast.net Port: 993 User Name: <my email address> Connection Security: SSL/TLS Authentication method: Normal Password

SMTP Server Name: smtp.comcast.net Port: 465 Connection Security: SSL/TLS Authentication method: Normal password User Name: <my email address>

content: Thunderbird is NOT an SMTP provider. Most Wordpress sites use plugins that provide that service. The eventual email might then be sent to an account where you use Thunderbird to access it, but your problem is within the Wordpress setup. I suggest you post your concerns on a Wordpress forum or solicit assistance from your plugin author page, assuming use of a plugin.
creator: davidsk

content:
Hello there Alian,katzen


Some of these email do not go to that email inbox but going to all mail? It seems to be that gmail is archiving or is using filters. Rather it s no thunderbird fault. A server sided issue “ skip the inbox “ filter .(archive it).

Looking at that fix Try to fix this with the repair folder option . Click on the inbox right click and choose properties and then that repair folder option.

Looking at the filters , gmail Check for email filters at gmail. Login at gmail via a browser , goto >filters>filters and blocked addresses>and check for filters that include the action>”skip the inbox” (archive it).

Archiving Check gmail inbox configuration : in gmail setting , look if you re not archiving new mail.

Check thunderbird messages Check thunderbird messages filters. Ensure that are no filters set to move mail to other folders. Go to >tools> message >filters.

unsubscribe from all mail(imap). In thunderbird > right click your account name >subscribe>uncheck the gmail/all mail folder .This prevents thunderbird from duplicating all inbox mail from into a separate folder.


You might try these steps written in text above from this paper. Question : are there duplicated emails , or missing parts of folders having disappeared new emails or just having that issue described above ? Are you using a Mac or a Windows computer
creator: Googlethunderbird.
